title: Avocado Toast with Chilli Oil

subs.name: whole-wheat roti (tawa-toasted) or multigrain bread for a more Indian-friendly base
hummus (chickpea paste) for a budget and avocado-unavailable swap
subs.notes: skip the mashing step; spread hummus directly

ingredients.name: olive oil or any neutral oil

subs.name: coconut oil, gingelly oil or any neutral cooking oil

steps.text: Toast the bread slices until golden and firm — in a toaster, on a tawa on medium flame, or in a pan with a scrape of oil. The toast must be crisp enough to hold the mash without going soggy.
Halve the avocados, remove the stones and scoop the flesh into a bowl. Add lemon juice, salt and black pepper. Mash roughly with a fork — some texture is pleasant, so do not over-mash into a puree.

In a tiny pan or ladle, warm the olive oil on low heat for 30 seconds. Add the red chilli powder and let it sizzle for 5 seconds to bloom the spice. Remove from heat immediately before the chilli burns.
Spread the avocado mash generously over the hot toast slices. Drizzle the chilli oil over each piece, scatter spring onion, and serve at once — the toast softens quickly once topped.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: avocado → hummus (chickpea paste) for a budget and avocado-unavailable swap (skip the mashing step; spread hummus directly); olive-oil → coconut oil, gingelly oil or any neutral cooking oil; bread → whole-wheat roti (tawa-toasted) or multigrain bread for a more Indian-friendly base; lemon → lime juice — same quantity
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Avocados are now available in most Indian metro supermarkets and in the south during March–July. Look for Hass avocados (dark-skinned); they should yield to gentle pressure when ripe. Toast bread directly on a medium tawa rather than in a toaster. The chilli oil can be made hotter with Kashmiri red chilli powder for colour and guntur powder for heat. Gingelly oil (nallennai) adds a distinctive South Indian note.
